rag_rules: Insert generated annex content only in the owner scope it was generated for, preserving the surrounding component or implementation structure unchanged.
For implementation-owned content, place it in implementation scope after the relevant structural declarations so existing subcomponents and connections remain intact.
Do not move implementation-specific content into a type scope or rely on type-level visibility rules when fusing implementation content.
generic_rules: Preserve all existing AADL declarations, formatting, and architecture elements outside the inserted annex block.
Use the input model as the authority for owner, scope, declared identifiers, and structural context during insertion.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Annex blocks use the AADL form `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation-level content belongs under the component implementation that owns the referenced structure, not under the component type.
anti_patterns: Do not insert content at a scope where referenced identifiers are not structurally visible.
Do not modify connections, features, subcomponents, or properties as part of annex fusion unless explicitly required by the generated content.
irrelevant_topics: Boolean expression examples unrelated to owner/scope fusion
Validator-driven datatype repairs for booleans, properties, or event ports when no diagnostics are active
